candidate.name: fellows_local_db (illustrative — see reference_designs/fellows_local_db for the real attestation)

summary.headline: Conformant where it counts: the cloud-LLM path is handled as a declared EX-CLOUD-LLM exception (AC-MCP-A), and the off-folder durability ceiling (CST-PWA-PRIVATE-SNAPSHOT) is honestly capability-reduced at the data layer (AC-1 / Goal-4). One MCP bulk-confirmation question is left for human review.

citations.note: install-time consent gate (recordMcpbConsent) + a persistent 'not a PNA right now' banner
the data-ops servers open both DBs mode=ro

evidence.detail: Maintainer review: cloud access is mediated by the EX-CLOUD-LLM exception handler (EX-H1 through EX-H8) — consent before the raise, a persistent non-PNA-mode signal, a runtime explainer, and a return-to-PNA-mode control.

Traced the consent path: a cloud client cannot reach Private DB rows without the workspace-side opt-in. EX-H7 (consent-to-human propagation) is best-effort via the MCP instructions handshake — an honest, attested gap, not over-claimed.

citations.note: _refuseDurablePrivateWriteOffFolder guards every mutation at the worker/data layer (not UI-only)

evidence.detail: No off-device egress vectors for private data; the allow-list covers only the magic-link auth origin.

CST-PWA-PRIVATE-SNAPSHOT is handled honestly: off Chromium the design refuses durable private writes at the data layer and says so, rather than promising a durable store it cannot keep (no false durability).

findings.rationale: The Communications MCP tool stages a mailto: URL rather than firing a transport (good), but whether every workspace path that launches it re-confirms the payload under all bulk-send flows could not be confirmed from the source alone — flagging for a human to exercise the bulk-send UI.
